summary: High-level architecture across Rx.NET, Ix.NET, System.Linq.Async, and AsyncRx with explicit push-vs-pull trade-offs.
recommendedPatterns: Choose IObservable<T> for push-based event streams and fan-out delivery.
Choose IEnumerable<T> or IAsyncEnumerable<T> when consumers should pull values on demand.
Document hot/cold, push/pull, and completion semantics at API boundaries.
avoidPatterns: Do not mix push and pull abstractions without a deliberate boundary.
Do not assume event-based code and reactive pipelines have identical lifetime semantics.
setupSteps: Identify whether the producer should push or the consumer should pull.
Pick the sequence abstraction before choosing operators or subjects.
Keep abstraction conversion seams explicit and testable.
commonPitfalls: Treating IAsyncEnumerable<T> as a drop-in replacement for broadcast event delivery.
Failing to describe completion semantics for long-lived reactive streams.
